summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Iteration 10 features: Multi-Event Comparison Simulation (POST /api/replay/compare), Trade Log CSV Export (GET /api/trades/export/csv), Price Alerts CRUD (POST/GET/DELETE /api/alerts), and PWA manifest.json. All 29 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified working with all new features functional.
passed_tests: Root endpoint: GET /api/ returns version 2.0.0
System status: GET /api/system/status returns running status, current_symbol, available_assets
Auth login: POST /api/auth/login with admin credentials works
Auth invalid: POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials returns 401
Multi-Event Comparison: POST /api/replay/compare with default config returns events_count, per_event[], aggregate{}
Multi-Event Comparison: POST /api/replay/compare with custom config works
Multi-Event Comparison: POST /api/replay/compare with specific event_ids works
CSV Export: GET /api/trades/export/csv returns text/csv content-type
CSV Export: GET /api/trades/export/csv has proper headers (Date, Symbol, Direction, Entry, Exit, PnL)
Price Alerts: GET /api/alerts returns alerts list
Price Alerts: POST /api/alerts creates alert with 'above' condition
Price Alerts: POST /api/alerts creates alert with 'below' condition
Price Alerts: POST /api/alerts with invalid symbol returns 400
Price Alerts: POST /api/alerts without target_price returns 400
Price Alerts: POST /api/alerts with invalid condition returns 400
Price Alerts: DELETE /api/alerts/{id} deactivates alert
Price Alerts: DELETE /api/alerts/{nonexistent} returns 404
Price Alerts: Created alerts appear in GET /api/alerts list
Replay events: GET /api/replay/events returns 8 historical events
Replay single: GET /api/replay/hormuz_2024 returns bars[], analytics{}, event{}
Replay simulate: POST /api/replay/simulate works correctly
Bot status: GET /api/bot/status returns enabled, min_confidence, scan_interval_sec
Fragility: GET /api/fragility returns score, level, size_multiplier
Options chain: GET /api/options/chain/CL returns options with greeks
Notifications: GET /api/notifications returns notifications list
Positions: GET /api/positions returns open positions list
Trades: GET /api/trades returns trade history
Assets: GET /api/assets returns CL, BZ, NG
PWA Manifest: GET /manifest.json returns valid PWA manifest with name, short_name, display, theme_color
Frontend: Replay tab shows Multi-Event Comparison panel
Frontend: Run Comparison button triggers comparison and shows results
Frontend: Comparison results show aggregate summary cards (Events, Total Trades, Win Rate, Aggregate PnL, Worst DD)
Frontend: Comparison results show PnL bar chart per event
Frontend: Comparison results show per-event table with all metrics
Frontend: Settings tab shows Price Alerts panel with create form
Frontend: Settings tab shows Trade Export panel with Download CSV button
Frontend: Price alert creation works (CL Above $95.50 created)
Frontend: Price alert deletion works
Frontend: Triggered alerts show TRIGGERED badge (CL Above $80)
Frontend: Dashboard Export CSV button visible in Recent Trades panel
Frontend: All existing features (Dashboard, Options, Portfolio, Backtest, Calendar) still work
seed_data_creation: Test alerts created during testing (CL Above $95.50, etc.)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 features fully functional. New features: 1) Multi-Event Comparison (POST /api/replay/compare) - runs strategy simulation across all 8 historical events and returns aggregate summary with per-event breakdown. 2) Trade CSV Export (GET /api/trades/export/csv) - downloads trade history as CSV file. 3) Price Alerts CRUD (POST/GET/DELETE /api/alerts) - create alerts for price above/below targets, alerts trigger when price crosses threshold. 4) PWA manifest.json configured. Frontend ReplayTab shows comparison panel with bar chart and table. Settings tab shows Price Alerts panel and Trade Export panel. Dashboard has Export CSV button in Recent Trades. All features from iterations 1-9 continue to work correctly.

mocked_apis.note: All trading is simulated via PaperBroker. Market data is simulated. Replay events use pre-defined trajectories. ML regime detection uses Emergent LLM Key.

trade_csv_export.response: CSV file download with headers: Date, Symbol, Direction, Entry Price, Exit Price, Quantity, PnL ($), Hold (min), Exit Reason
